title: Build core Busy AI pages from sketches + BrandBoost references (simplified)

requirements.text: Create the app shell (responsive) with a left sidebar navigation inspired by the uploaded BrandBoost screenshots, including routes/pages for: Chat, Photo/Video Maker, Business Analytics, Gallery, and Shop Maker (placeholder).

quotes: Start building all the pages with the information I have gave at starting

acceptanceCriteria: A persistent left sidebar is visible on desktop and collapses on small screens.
Sidebar contains nav items: Chat, Photo/Video Maker, Business Analytics, Gallery, Shop Maker.
Each nav item routes to a working page with a header/title matching the selected module.

requirements.text: Apply a consistent professional visual theme across all pages using a navy/green/black gradient background direction with modern typography and subtle glow accents (no blue-purple heavy palette), matching the overall style of the uploaded references.

quotes: I want a navy gradient blue or green or black like that color which will be matching u can use that

acceptanceCriteria: All pages share the same background gradient family (navy/green/black) and consistent spacing/typography.
Primary buttons have a subtle glow/hover effect without being distracting.
No page uses a drastically different visual style from the rest of the app.

requirements.text: Implement a lightweight butterfly-flying background animation that appears on every page without blocking UI interactions and with an option to reduce motion (respect prefers-reduced-motion).

quotes: Every pages I want butterflies flying

acceptanceCriteria: Butterfly animation is visible on all routes/pages.
Animation does not prevent clicking/typing (pointer events do not block UI).
When the OS/browser prefers-reduced-motion is enabled, the animation is disabled or significantly reduced.

requirements.text: Implement authentication using Internet Identity, and provide a dedicated login screen styled similarly to the user’s sketch (simple centered card), plus a logged-in state indicator in the app shell.

quotes: Start building all the pages with the information I have gave at starting

acceptanceCriteria: Unauthenticated users are routed to a login screen that offers Internet Identity sign-in.
Authenticated users can access all main pages.
The UI shows the logged-in status (e.g., principal short form) and a logout action.

requirements.text: Build the Chat page UI based on the BrandBoost-style layout: message list area, message input, and basic chat controls (New Chat, Clear). Implement simple non-AI responses (rule-based or canned) to keep the build lightweight.

quotes: Start building all the pages with the information I have gave at starting

acceptanceCriteria: Chat page contains a scrollable message history and a text input with send button.
New Chat clears the current conversation; Clear removes messages.
Sending a message results in an assistant reply generated locally/with simple backend logic (no external LLM).
Chat history persists per authenticated user (at least for the current app session; preferably stored in backend).

requirements.text: Build the Photo/Video Maker landing page with a grid of feature cards inspired by the uploaded BrandBoost maker screenshots. Include at minimum: Photo for Post and Video for Reels/Shorts, with “Get Started” leading to their respective flows.

quotes: Start building all the pages with the information I have gave at starting

acceptanceCriteria: Maker landing page shows at least two cards: Photo for Post and Video for Reels/Shorts.
Each card includes a title, short description, and a Get Started CTA.
Get Started navigates to a working page for the selected tool.

requirements.text: Implement the Photo for Post flow based on the references: a form page to enter business details (company name, category, product/service, offer, address/contact, additional details, optional image upload), then a theme selection step, then a result page that renders a professional-looking poster using templates (client-side rendering).

quotes: the videos and photo that is to be generated has to be proffessional and attractive for professional use

acceptanceCriteria: Photo tool includes a details form with the fields shown in the BrandBoost reference screenshots (or close equivalents).
User can pick a template/theme and see a generated poster preview on the result screen.
Generated poster looks visually structured (headline, subhead, accent blocks) and is exportable (download as PNG).

requirements.text: Implement the Video for Reels/Shorts flow based on the user sketch: details form (business name, service/product, offers, plus optional media), a transitions/style selection step (simple choices), and a result preview that plays a short template animation (client-side) and allows export (at least as a downloadable MP4/GIF if feasible, otherwise provide a downloadable image sequence/placeholder export).

quotes: the videos and photo that is to be generated has to be proffessional and attractive for professional use

acceptanceCriteria: Video tool includes a details form and a transitions/style selection step.
Result screen shows an animated preview (template-based).
An export action exists; if true video export is not feasible within constraints, the UI clearly downloads a placeholder artifact (e.g., GIF or frames ZIP) and labels it accordingly.

requirements.text: Build the Business Analytics module inspired by the uploaded BrandBoost analytics screen: a financial input form (initial investment, total revenue, total costs + cost breakdown fields) and an analytics report view with computed metrics (profit, margin) plus simple charts (bar/pie) rendered client-side.

quotes: I want business analytics as per the photo provided

acceptanceCriteria: Analytics page contains a form for initial investment, revenue, costs, and cost breakdown fields.
Clicking Generate Analytics shows computed results (at least profit and profit margin).
Charts (bar and/or pie) render based on the entered values.

requirements.text: Create a Gallery page inspired by the uploaded BrandBoost gallery screenshot with filter tabs (All, Photos, Videos, Analytics) and cards listing generated outputs with actions to view and download.

quotes: Start building all the pages with the information I have gave at starting

acceptanceCriteria: Gallery shows filter tabs for at least All, Photos, Videos, Analytics.
Each generated item appears as a card with title/type/date and actions (View, Download).
At least metadata for generated items is persisted per authenticated user in the backend and reloaded on refresh.

requirements.text: Implement backend persistence (single Motoko actor) for per-user data needed by the simplified app: chat conversations (messages), gallery items (metadata), and saved analytics reports (inputs + computed outputs).

quotes: Start building all the pages with the information I have gave at starting

acceptanceCriteria: Backend stores data keyed by authenticated user principal.
Frontend can create/read lists for chats and gallery items via canister calls.
Analytics reports can be saved and retrieved for the Gallery filter (Analytics).

requirements.text: Add a Shop Maker placeholder page based on the sketch card (‘Shop maker’) that explains it is coming soon and includes a simple contact/info card layout (no e-commerce or payments).

quotes: On my sketches buy some major things to be combined as a single app

acceptanceCriteria: Shop Maker route exists and is reachable from the sidebar.
Page displays a polished ‘Coming soon’ message and a simple info/contact card section.

constraints: Use English for all user-facing UI text.
Backend must follow single-actor Motoko architecture (all logic in the main actor).
Do not implement email/password authentication; use Internet Identity only.
Do not integrate external LLM or AI model providers; implement template-based generation and simple canned chat replies instead to keep scope small and within platform limits.
Frontend paths designated immutable by the platform must not be edited (compose UI using allowed files/components).
nonGoals: True AI image generation via third-party model APIs.
True AI video generation via third-party model APIs.
Payments, subscriptions, or full e-commerce features.
Complex multi-step onboarding beyond a basic Internet Identity login screen.
